title: X-Wing Marks the Spot

{{Quote|Yes, yes, Artoo, I know it's necessary to use that treasure to purchase new X-wing fighters for the Rebellion. What I don't understand is why it's necessary for me to stay here with Chewbacca and Q-7N, while you go off with Master Luke, Captain Solo and Princess Leia!|C-3PO|<ref name="xwing">{{Kids1997Cite|issue1=6|issue2=10|story=X-Wing Marks the Spot}}</ref>}}
"'''X-Wing Marks the Spot'''" is a five part comic written by [[Mike W. Barr]] and illustrated by [[Ken Steacy]] and [[Glen Mullaly]], and was published in issues ''#6-#10'' of the magazine [[Star Wars Kids (1997)|''Star Wars Kids'']]. It tells the story of how the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] purchased new [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] to replace those lost at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Plot summary==
After receiving 500,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from [[Sprool]] the Trader for a crate of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] treasure, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] set off for the [[Incom Factory]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. When they enter the system of the unknown [[Planet/Legends|planet]] on which the factory is located, they receive an advertisement from a certain [[Vors Voorhorian|"Gears" Gilhooey]] from a nearby beacon. Leia realizes that the advert is a message for those intending to buy the T-65 [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]], and orders Han to follow the coordinates sent by the advert.

The coordinates take them to an unknown [[Planet/Legends|planet]], where Han is confronted by "Gears". "Gears" bests Han at [[Unarmed combat/Legends|hand-to-hand combat]], and reveals his true identity—[[Vors Voorhorian]], the designer of the X-wing. He takes them to the Incom factory, where Leia is taken to a conference room to negotiate price.

[[File:Lukedestroyfighter_web.jpg|thumb|left|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] destroys a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] using only his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].]]

Meanwhile, Han and Luke, along with Artoo, discover that the X-wings in the Incom Hangar are nothing more than empty shells. Setting off to confront Voorhorian, they are confronted by four guards. The three manage to disarm the guards and burst into the conference room. Voorhorian sets them straight, informing them that even the Incom Factory might not be safe from the Empire. At that very moment, four Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] attack the base.

Fearing destruction if they take off in the ''Falcon'', Luke causes a distraction by using a piece of flexible metal as a trampoline, flipping through the air and cleaving through the underbelly of the lead fighter with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. The damaged TIE crashed into another, buying the ''Falcon'' time to escape.

While flying away from the planet, Voorhorian directed them towards an old, hollow, derelict [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]]. He revealed the contents: an entire fleet of X-wings crammed in tightly. Just as the fighters were revealed, the remaining TIEs found the transport, and attacked it. The Rebels acted quickly. Han boarded the ''Falcon'' and used it to ward off the fighters while Leia and Voorhorian drained power from the new X-wings to get the transport operational. Due to the GR-75's lack of proper armament, the Rebels improvised and caused the TIEs to crash into the ship's shields. The ''Falcon'' then towed the transport back to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].
